data.body: PREPLANNING_CONTEXT: {"version":1,"epicId":"78fc-3858","generatedAt":"2026-04-19T20:02:44.305061Z","generatedBy":"preplanning","epic":{"title":"Reduce ticket CLI subprocess overhead via sourceable ticket-lib API","description":"Bash-native rehome of ticket CLI dispatcher; sourceable ticket-lib-api.sh; workflow latency <2-20s per turn is the primary target.","successCriteria":["Writes <0.05s","Reads <0.15s","Dispatcher thin wrapper","Sourcing side-effect-free","Flock preserved","Existing tests pass + regression suite",">=60% aggregate workflow reduction","Cross-platform + shell-special-chars + DSO_TICKET_LEGACY rollback"]},"stories":[{"id":"79f3-c7a6","title":"As a performance engineer, I can compare new ticket CLI latency against a reliable pre-refactor baseline","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["tests/fixtures/ticket-cli-baseline.json exists with per-op mean/p50/p95/stddev for 9 hot ops","Reusable benchmark runner script produces same JSON shape for Story G","Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic"],"considerations":["[Testing] Reference hardware documented alongside baseline"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":[],"satisfiesCriterion":"A new regression test suite measures per-call latency for each covered operation"},{"id":"564c-e391","title":"As a developer, the ticket CLI dispatcher init runs without spawning Python processes, with a rollback flag for safety","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["_ensure_initialized spawns zero python3 processes on default path","Bash-native sync-marker hash uses md5 truncated to 12 hex chars, byte-identical to current Python output","DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1 routes to pre-refactor Python path","Read ops complete in <0.15s per call on Linux reference hardware","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Performance] Must shave ~160-300ms from every call","[Reliability] Sync-marker hash cross-process compatible with existing /tmp/.ticket-sync-* format"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["79f3-c7a6"],"satisfiesCriterion":"Read-path operations complete in <0.15s per call"},{"id":"b591-d4ec","title":"As a developer, ticket show runs via a sourceable bash library function in under 0.15s on Linux","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["ticket-lib-api.sh exists and defines ticket_show","Dispatcher for ticket show sources the library and calls the function (no exec)","ticket show completes in <0.15s on Linux reference hardware","Sourceability contract enforced: no set -e bleed, no exit, no trap EXIT leakage, no GIT_* mutation","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] Library design exposes conventional extension pattern for downstream epics","[Testing] Dispatcher exec->source architectural shift; _ticketlib_dispatch wraps in subshell"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["564c-e391"],"satisfiesCriterion":"The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions"},{"id":"29e5-0a74","title":"As a developer, write_commit_event runs bash-native with preserved flock semantics and byte-exact JSON event output","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["write_commit_event spawns zero python3 processes on default path","Flock contract preserved verbatim (LOCK_EX, 30s x 2 retries)","JSON byte-for-byte identical to json.dump(ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':'), sort_keys=True) over 50+ random inputs","emit-review-event.sh + suggestion-record.sh pass integration tests","Read-after-write consistency test passes","DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1 routes to pre-refactor Python path","Write ops complete in <0.05s on Linux reference hardware","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Reliability] ticket-flock-contract.md authoritative; Story H updates doc","[Reliability] Reducer cache invalidation survives bash-native write path","[Consumer Impact] emit-review-event.sh + suggestion-record.sh not ticket subcommands","[Performance] SC1 target for reference Linux; Alpine/macOS get slack budget in Story G"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["564c-e391"],"satisfiesCriterion":"Write-path operations complete in <0.05s per call"},{"id":"7f2a-8fa4","title":"As a developer, all hot ticket CLI ops dispatch through the sourceable library with no logic duplication","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["Library exposes ticket_create, tag, untag, comment, edit (pure bash); link, list, transition (thin wrapper)","Dispatcher routes all 9 covered subcommands through _ticketlib_dispatch","Pure-bash ops <0.05s; thin-wrapper ops <0.15s on Linux reference hardware","Each rehomed op honors DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1","plugins/dso/docs/ticket-lib-api-extension-pattern.md published","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] Single source of truth for each op","[Performance] Op-class split makes SC1/SC2 targets meaningful per op"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["b591-d4ec","29e5-0a74"],"satisfiesCriterion":"The CLI dispatcher sources the library and delegates all covered operations to library functions - no logic duplication"},{"id":"da60-3f54","title":"As a developer, a regression suite verifies that sourcing the ticket library never leaks side effects into my shell","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["Sourceability regression test suite covers every library function in CI","Strict-mode caller (set -euo pipefail, trap EXIT preset) not affected by sourcing","Subshell-wrap coverage for functions using local set -e or traps","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Testing] Fixture test harness runs under strict mode","[Maintainability] Regression suite is standing guard"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["7f2a-8fa4"],"satisfiesCriterion":"Sourcing the library is side-effect-free to the caller's shell"},{"id":"74e1-469e","title":"As a developer, the ticket CLI works correctly on Linux, macOS bash 3.2+, and Alpine/BusyBox matrix","Shell-special-char round-trip tests pass (quotes, backslashes, newlines, unicode)","Flock fallback: hard flock(1) on Linux+macOS; python3 fcntl.flock fallback on Alpine with Story-G slack budget","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Testing] Platform matrix runs on every CI build","[Reliability] Alpine slack budget trade-off documented"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["da60-3f54"],"satisfiesCriterion":"The library operates correctly across results captured for epic closing comment","tests/scripts/test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh passes unmodified","Unit tests written and passing"],"considerations":["[Testing] CI runner variance handled via statistical thresholds","[Performance] Per-platform slack for macOS/Alpine avoids false failures"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["74e1-469e"],"satisfiesCriterion":"A representative /dso:sprint workflow trace demonstrates >=60% aggregate
